summary: Built-in Jolt Physics colliding shape index is always 0

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: v4.4.stable.official [4c311cbee] with built-in Jolt Physics
- Not reproducible in: v4.3.stable.official [77dcf97d8] with Godot Jolt 0.15.0 Extension from AssetLib

### System information

Godot v4.3.stable - Garuda Linux #1 ZEN SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC Fri, 07 Mar 2025 20:18:44 +0000 - X11 - GLES3 (Compatibility) - Mesa Intel(R) HD Graphics 630 (KBL GT2) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-7700HQ CPU @ 2.80GHz (8 Threads)

### Issue description

Issue: When using Godot 4.4 with built-in Jolt Physics, `shape` value in the `get_rest_info()` output dictionary (obtained as ShapeCast3D's `collision_result`) is 0 regardless of the actual shape with which ShapeCast3D collided. 

Expected: different shapes have different shape indexes.

This issue does not occur when using DEFAULT physics on Godot 4.4, and it also doesnt occur when using  Godot Jolt extension or DEFAULT physics on Godot 4.3 version.

### Steps to reproduce

- Open MRP
- Set project's physics engine to Jolt
- Launch the game, note the output in the terminal being all 0 (three collision shapes report three different shapes of the same rigid body have index 0)
- Change project's physics engine to DEFAULT
- Launch the game, the output in the terminal should read 0 1 2 (three different shapes have different indexes)

Identical setup in a 4.3 project using the Godot Jolt Extension produces 0 1 2 as expected.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[ShapeTest4.4.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19436320/ShapeTest4.4.zip)

description.text:

Mac / Android / Godot 4.4

Did anyone had the same issue?

### Steps to reproduce

```gdscript
print("Initial accel test: ", Input.get_accelerometer())
print("Initial gravity test: ", Input.get_gravity())
```

```gdscript
extends Node3D

@onready var camera = $Camera3D
var gyro_sensitivity = 2.0
var base_rotation = Vector3.ZERO
var gyro_enabled = false  # Manual toggle for testing

func _ready():
    print("SCRIPT IS RUNNING!")
    camera.current = true
    if has_node("MeshInstance3D"):
        $MeshInstance3D.position = Vector3(0, 0, -2)
        print("Box positioned at: ", $MeshInstance3D.position)
    else:
        print("ERROR: No MeshInstance3D found!")
    if not has_node("DirectionalLight3D"):
        var light = DirectionalLight3D.new()
        light.position = Vector3(0, 5, 5)
        light.rotation_degrees = Vector3(-45, 0, 0)
        add_child(light)
        print("Added DirectionalLight3D to scene.")
    print("Scene setup complete. Waiting 2 seconds to test sensors...")
    await get_tree().create_timer(2.0).timeout
    print("Initial gyro test: ", Input.get_gyroscope())
    print("Initial accel test: ", Input.get_accelerometer())
    print("Initial gravity test: ", Input.get_gravity())

        var gyro = Input.get_gyroscope()

            print("Gyro values: ", gyro)
            var rotation_change = Vector3(
                gyro.x * gyro_sensitivity,
                gyro.y * gyro_sensitivity,
                gyro.z * gyro_sensitivity
            )
            camera.rotation += rotation_change * delta
            camera.rotation.x = clamp(camera.rotation.x, -PI/2, PI/2)
            camera.rotation.y = clamp(camera.rotation.y, -PI/2, PI/2)
            print("Camera rotation (degrees): ", camera.rotation_degrees)
        else:

        # Log other sensors for comparison
        var accel = Input.get_accelerometer()
        if accel != Vector3.ZERO:
```

summary: Fonts are scaling inconsistently using canvas_items scaling.

description.text:
### Tested versions

4.4, 4.3

### System information

Windows 11

### Issue description

I am working on a UI based game. I have some dialog sitting in a scroll view. When changing window size the amount of text shown unexpectedly changes. To illustrate the issue here is an image of the relevant area in my MRP and the same section after maximizing the window. A whole line of text is lost. 

Using viewport scaling is a non starter because it turns everything into a pixelated mess. Adjusting hinting does not fix it.

For UI designers this is a non-trivial issue. Modern devices can range from 720p to 4k. Knowing exactly how big a tooltip will be or how a dialog will fit in a dialog window is can be important and should have consistent scaling with the rest of the UI. 

Note: I submitted this issue for 4.3 a few weeks ago and it made it to triage before being deleted. Not closed, deleted.

Open my MRP and hit play with an external window. Then maximize the window to a resolution greater than 1080p. I am using a 1440p monitor. If your monitor is 1080p, you will need to adjust the project window resolution to be 720p to see similar changes.

summary: Reparenting Control node with a Control child in child_entered_tree crashes the game without any info

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproducable in 4.4.stable, 4.3.stable, 4.2.2.stable, 

### System information

Godot v4.4.stable - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3070 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.7242) - 12th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-12700K (20 threads)

### Issue description

When connecting to the child_entered_tree signal, and inside that connecting function you reparent the Control node that just entered the tree to a different node, the game crashes if the newly added child node has another Control node as a child of itself. 

Also when the game then crashes, there is no warning or anything else in the Output or Debugger, so it is quite difficult to find out what even happened or what caused the crash.

Note: The game does not crash if:
- you reparent the child node deferred (the best workaround for now)
- the child node has no children of its own
- the child node has children of its own that are NOT Control nodes

The log:
[godot.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19428262/godot.log)

### Steps to reproduce

- Connect to the child_entered_tree signal of a Control Node (I will call this "Container"), call it e.g. `_on_child_entered_tree(node: Node)`
- Have some other Control node `some_other_control_node`
- Inside the Container's `_on_child_entered_tree` function, call `node.reparent(some_other_control_node)`
- Now, add some Control node to the first Container, this new child should have a child of its own.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)
Here is a MRP:
[entered_tree_reparent.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19430799/entered_tree_reparent.zip)
Start the main.tscn, and then press `ui_accept` (e.g. Enter) to crash the game.

>Old MPR, for archival purposes, but just use the new one:
[entered_tree_reparent.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19428277/entered_tree_reparent.zip)

Note: For the child node to be added, I made a custom scene. And I also included some scene files without Control child but plain Node child, and one without any children. These do not crash the game, maybe they are useful for testing purposes.

summary: Scene inheritance is broken.

description.text:
### Tested versions

4.4

### System information

Win 11 - i5 13600kf - rtx 4070

### Issue description

Multi level scene inheritance is just broken in the editor. Chanes often don't propagate. Often times it's needed to open all the scenes in the inheritance chain, then change one scene in the chain where you want the change to start, save and that might change the scenes up the chain too (but not always, only sometimes!). If you make a change without having the middle scenes open too the change doesn't propagate to the last scene in the inheritance (or any scenes in the chain until you open them all).

Basically imagine a scene inheritance like this:
Scene A -> B -> C -> D

If you make a change in the scene A then scenes C and D will not change.
To propagate the change from A  to C, you need to open B and then open C (close it before you open it if you have it already open).
To propagate from A to D you need to close everything, change A, save then open B, then open C, then open D.

**This doesn't always work either, sometimes changes don't propagate at all ever meaning the inheritance becomes completely useless.**

Video: https://youtu.be/MiA_rVxg2WU

### Steps to reproduce

In the MRP, open the scene First and Third, hide the node inside the First and observe no changes to the Third scene. Now close the third scene, open the Second scene and then open the Third scene and now the changes either propagated all the way to the Third scene or just did not.

The inheritance in the MRP is First -> Second -> Third scenes.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[InheritanceMrp.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19427944/InheritanceMrp.zip)

summary: Scripts are not properly reloaded

description.text:
### Tested versions

Godot 4.4


### System information

Windows 10

### Issue description

I'm one of the maintainers of the Godot Kotlin/JVM module.
Since the 4.4 release, our scripts are no longer properly reloaded by the editor when modified from an external IDE (which is basically always the case with Intellij being the official Kotlin IDE).

I did some testing, and they are properly reloaded if they are already open in Godot's own script editor (and then receive the prompt asking if we want to reload the file. The editor setting that reloads script on external change is set to `True`). But when the scripts are not open but still loaded (because used in a scene for example), they are never reloaded automatically, despite the editor file system detecting the changes. Even worse, if we open them in Godot script editor after an external change, the source code appearing is not up-to-date either so it seems this script editor only detect changes while the script is open, but doesn't try to reload them on opening.

GDScript works perfectly fine, even when not open in the script editor, so I hooked a debugger and searched for what functions are actually reloading it. 
I found out that EditorFileSystem::_update_script_documentation is doing it. 
https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/0595bb8a4219f6f02dedcca8b0a65e5db29992d8/editor/editor_file_system.cpp#L2186
Our module doesn't support exporting documentation to Godot yet, so that function can never reload our script, and it's probably not meant to be the only way a script is supposed to be reloaded outside the script editor.
We can temporary fix that behavior by implementing a dummy documentation support but it's just a workaround.
It was perfectly working in Godot 4.3, so probably some changes broke that behaviour.

### Steps to reproduce

- Create a simple GDScript file
- Use a debugger and put a break point here: https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/0595bb8a4219f6f02dedcca8b0a65e5db29992d8/editor/editor_file_system.cpp#L2187
- Open Godot and load a scene with the script attached (without opening it in the script editor)
- Modify the script in an external program
- Notice that the documentation update method is the one currently reloading the script.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

-

summary: Canvas items content scale mode doesn't work in embedded subwindows

description.text:
### Tested versions

v4.4.stable.official [4c311cbee], v4.4.1.rc2.official [abef5e0d2], v4.5.dev1.official [97241ffea]

### System information

Godot v4.4.stable - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - integrated AMD Radeon(TM) Graphics (Advanced Micro Devices, Inc.; 31.0.21916.2) - AMD Ryzen 7 5700U with Radeon Graphics (16 threads)

### Issue description

When a subwindow is both embedded and set to the Canvas Items content scale mode, the contents of the window are not scaled at all. The subwindow will scale its contents as expected if it isn't embedded (e.g. if Embed Subwindows is unchecked in project settings), or if the Viewport content scale mode is used instead.

This happens in the Forward+, Mobile, and Compability renderers.

### Steps to reproduce

### Issue description

`AudioStreamPlaylist` ignores the `pitch_scale` value; Playback is always at default 1.0 speed. This is true whether `pitch_scale` is changed mid-scene, or if the scene starts with a custom value for `pitch_scale.` It does not matter if it is changed in the Inspector or via code.

[Calinou notes](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/104527#issuecomment-2748906596) this is because the `p_rate_scale` is hard coded to 1.0:

[godot/modules/interactive_music/audio_stream_playlist.cpp](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/bf1c2583f4a13d501ec9eae58912f580fb5484ef/modules/interactive_music/audio_stream_playlist.cpp#L340) - Line 340 in [bf1c258](/godotengine/godot/commit/bf1c2583f4a13d501ec9eae58912f580fb5484ef)
```
 playback[idx]->mix(mix_buffer + i, 1.0, to_mix - i); // Fill rest of mix buffer 
```

### Steps to reproduce

1. Add any type of AudioStreamPlayer node to the scene
2. Set its Stream to the AudioStreamPlaylist type and add audio to its Streams array.
3. Set the `pitch_scale` to a value other than 1.
4. Run the scene, or play the audio in the Inspector. It will ignore the set `pitch_scale` value and play at default speed.
5. Change the `pitch_scale` in the Inspector or via code. It will ignore the set `pitch_scale` value and play at default speed.

When rendering the contents of a SubViewport to a ViewportTexture on a material of a mesh, the SubViewport renders nodes that are not its descendants. I noticed this while trying to figure out why a SubViewport was rendering a non-child Camera3D, but I have yet to replicate that issue.

3. Add a StandardMaterial to the quad mesh and make it a local resource. Set the _albedo_texture_ to be a _ViewportTexture_, to which set the SubViewport as the _viewport_path_.

summary: the env property of CharFXTransform does not automatically convert to int.

description.text:
- *Production edit: See https://github.com/godotengine/godot-docs-user-notes/discussions/210#discussioncomment-12584036.*

### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: 4.4.stable.

### System information

Godot v4.4.stable (4c311cbee) - Nobara Linux 41 (GNOME Edition) on Wayland - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1050 (nvidia; 570.124.04) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-8750H CPU @ 2.20GHz (12 threads)

### Issue description

The `env` property of `CharFXTransform` is a dictionary of the arguments passed into the bbcode tag corresponding to a custom `RichTextEffect`.  
According to the [documentation page](https://docs.godotengine.org/en/stable/classes/class_charfxtransform.html#class-charfxtransform-property-env), it is supposed to automatically convert the values to `bool`, `int` or `float` when their content matches, however I found by testing that integer values will be converted to float, not int.

### Steps to reproduce

Create a script inheriting `CharFXTransform`:
```gdscript
extends RichTextEffect
class_name TestEffect

var bbcode := "test"

var processed = false

func _process_custom_fx(char_fx: CharFXTransform) -> bool:
	if processed: return true
	for key in char_fx.env:
		var val = char_fx.env[key]
		print("{0}: {1} ({2})".format([key, val, type_string(typeof(val))]))
	processed = true
	return true
```

Create a Scene with a `RichTextLabel`, add `TestEffect` to its `custom_effects` property, check `bbcode_enabled` and add some text containing a test tag, like this: `[test int_var=2 float_var=0.5]text` (some text after the tag is necessary for `_process_custom_fx` to be called).  
You can change the `layout_mode` to "Anchors" and the `anchors_preset` to "Full Rect" to actually see the text but it's not necessary.

Running the scene with the given text will print out:
```log
int_var: 2.0 (float)
float_var: 0.5 (float)
```

We can see that the `int_var` value that should be converted to int was converted to float instead.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[CharFXTransform bug MRP.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19408080/CharFXTransform.bug.MRP.zip)

description.text:
### Tested versions

4.4, 4.5.dev

### System information

Godot v4.5.dev.mono - EndeavourOS  SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC Fri, 07 Mar 2025 20:19:00 +0000 on Wayland - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Mobile) - integrated AMD Radeon Graphics (RADV RENOIR) - AMD Ryzen 7 4800U with Radeon Graphics (16 threads)

### Issue description

The texture size override only works during the first startup for Sprite2D. If you set the size override again and call `queue_redraw`, the Sprite2D will not be updated.

### Steps to reproduce

```gdscript
@tool
extends Sprite2D

@export var speed:int=100
@export var size_override:= Vector2i(128,128):
	set(value):
		size_override=value
		RenderingServer.texture_set_size_override(
			texture.get_rid(),size_override.x,size_override.y)
		queue_redraw()
	get:
		return size_override
@export var proc := false

func _process(delta: float) -> void:
	if not proc: return
	var d  = int(speed*delta)
	size_override+=Vector2i(d,d)
	print("size override: ",size_override)
	if size_override.x>=256:
		size_override=Vector2(64,64)
	queue_redraw()
```
Change `size_override` or trun on `proc`, the Sprite2D doesn't update both in editor and running game.
Sprite2D only updates if restart the editor or game.

summary: [Web] load_threaded_get_status returns THREAD_LOAD_IN_PROGRESS, but file is finished loading

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in 4.4.1-rc2

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.rc2 - Linux Mint 22.1 (Xia) on X11 - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 2 monitors - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4060 Ti (nvidia; 550.120) - AMD Ryzen 9 3900X 12-Core Processor (24 threads)

### Issue description

If in a Web export both Extension Support and Thread Support are checked, then if you try to check the status of a load_threaded_request() by using load_threaded_get_status() then it will always return THREAD_LOAD_IN_PROGRESS even though the file is loaded and can be retrieved with load_threaded_get(). This creates an impossible situation as the documentation tells us we should always check the status before getting the file or it will block, but unfortunately the status is stuck at loading with the progress being 0%.
The way this should work is if the file is truly ready then any call to load_threaded_get_status() should return THREAD_LOAD_LOADED. 

Going Further if you un-tick Thread Support in the export menu then the background loading works as expected, because progress reaches 100.0%..
If you re-tick Thread Support but now un-tick Extensions Support in the export menu then the background loading works as expected, because progress reaches 100.0%.

### Steps to reproduce

summary: Add inspector property reordering & regroup CameraAttribute auto_exposure props

description.text:
<!--
Please target the `master` branch in priority.

Relevant fixes are cherry-picked for stable branches as needed by maintainers.

To speed up the contribution process and avoid CI errors, please set up pre-commit hooks locally:
https://docs.godotengine.org/en/latest/contributing/development/code_style_guidelines.html
-->
Fixes #101467. This adds general support for reordering properties in the inspector. There's an extra parameter in the hint string for `PROPERTY_USAGE_GROUP` and `PROPERTY_USAGE_SUBGROUP`. So there can be up to 3 comma-separated elements:
1. (Unchanged) The prefix every property in the group should have
2. (Unchanged) The indentation level of the group
3. (New) A value of either 1, -1, or 0. 
0 does nothing. If it's a 1 or -1, the group will instead send all its properties to a different property, whose name is the group's name. A value of 1 will put them below the destination property, and -1 will put them above it

The new feature is used to fix duplicate groups in `CameraAttributesPhysical` & `CameraAttributesPractical`
| Before (from issue) | After |
| - | - |
| ![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/74f4ae19-4ed4-423f-9317-c26b6e23f81c) | ![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/5b786fd3-afc5-4df7-83c1-79d935a430c3) |

Here's how the group definition changed:
```c++
// Before
ADD_GROUP("Auto Exposure", "auto_exposure_");
// After
ADD_GROUP("auto_exposure_speed", "auto_exposure_,0,1");
```
So rather than having another group called "Auto Exposure", all the properties that would normally belong to that group, are instead transported to under the property called "auto_exposure_speed". Letting you add to parent groups, from a child class

In GDScript it would look like this:
```gdscript
@export_group("auto_exposure_speed", "auto_exposure,0,1")
```


<details>
<summary>Old implementation</summary>

~~Rather than adding additional methods to Object itself, I opted to allow it inside `_validate_property` or `_get_property_list`, through `EditorInspector` from `EditorInterface`.~~

~~You first check if it's running in the editor, then you get the inspector and call `set_reorder_prop`~~

```c++
if (Engine::get_singleton()->is_editor_hint()) {
	EditorInspector *inspector = EditorInterface::get_singleton()->get_inspector();

	if (inspector) {
		inspector->set_reorder_prop(p_property.name, "auto_exposure_enabled");
	}
}
```
</details>

summary: Window can end up offscreen when leaving fullscreen mode
Allow the `filename_filter` property to be changed.

description.text:
This fixes #104067 in a hacky way.

![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/bd9cb590-ced5-4b7a-9b21-f1522236a20f)
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/bd2ed2a3-9a5d-44a0-96b7-ff8a467d0021)
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/910f6828-c738-4d79-ac11-7f4f00da85fc)

A downside of this PR is that if the property is hidden away using this code
```gdscript
@tool
extends FileDialog

func _validate_property(property:Dictionary):
	if property.name == "filename_filter":
		property.usage = PROPERTY_USAGE_NO_EDITOR
```
The `filename_filter` property persists and still works. I could not find a fix for this, but suggestions are welcome. Using the code in commit `14f519f47425a2062542f1f70aa68622be617e88` does not work due to some unknown reason. I even tried doing it like this
```cpp
	if (!filename_filter_box->is_visible()) {
		file_name_filter.clear();
	}
```
However that failed to provide the desired effect. As of now, it seems that removing the faulty code is the only way to proceed. 

It appears that using `!show_filename_filter` makes the box permanently uneditable as it is being cleared all the time, and using `show_filename_filter` allows editing but does not clear the box when it is hidden away then un-hidden. Truly quite a deep problem. I couldn't figure out how to print messages so that I could debug the state of `show_filename_filter`, and exactly pinpoint what was going wrong.

NOTE : Ready to merge.

summary: `EditorResourcePicker`: Add `Quick Load` button

description.text:
Restore part of functionality of reverted PR https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/97860. 
As I see from discussion, several concerns were raised about the original changes:
- The viewable area became too short when the field was not empty (https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/97860#pullrequestreview-2350629401, https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/97860#issuecomment-2599105410)
- Load options were removed from dropdown menu.

As described in https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/102196:

> User feedback has shown that this change isn't always a clear improvement, because:
> 
> 1. The icon eats up horizontal space that's already limited here, cutting the text or previews significantly.
> 2. The "Load" functionality was completely removed, while it's still useful for many.
> 3. It breaks user expectations to no longer have load options next to Clear/Save/etc.

In this PR:
 - (1) is addressed by only displaying the "Quick Load" button when the field is empty.
 - (2) and (3) are avoided entirely, as the existing code for load options is preserved.
 
So now current functionality has not degraded and at the same time initial setup for empty field became quicker. While this may be a not complete solution, it is still usability improvement (Perfect is the enemy of good).

I'm sure there could be other opinions, so feedback is welcome.

Preview:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/161f897f-88f3-4a0d-883f-673493b5516f

### Issue description

- When adding animation keys in the `AnimationPlayer` using the menu editor, the editor experiences a progressive freezing or slowdown. The issue becomes more pronounced as more keys are added. Unlike a complete crash, the editor remains responsive but increasingly sluggish.

- In the AnimationPlayer, create other animation.
- In other animation add multiple animation keys via the menu editor.
- Observe the progressive freezing and slowdown as more keys are added.

summary: Fix excessively calling resized and floating point errors in Control.

description.text:
This introduces two new booleans approx_pos_changed and approx_size_changed which are the pos_changed and size_changed booleans that are immune to floating point errors.

This aims to fix two bugs in one shot. #93832 and #48935.


This is a re-make of #104173 , as I had ruined the commit history due to my lack of experience with git and github in general. Apologies.

All in all, the fix is the same. This builds upon the hack that is used to do `set_size()`. Again, this splits the logic in such a way that the `resized` is called only if the approx position is changed, but the position and size is changed regardless of any approximations. This is a bodge on a bodge. Ideally `set_size()` should be tweaked to fix this error. 

Here's how they work in action. I wrote a script with the following GDScript
```
extends Control

func _ready() -> void:
	size = Vector2(120, 40.0003)
	print("start => ", size)
	size.y = 40
	print("40 => ", size)
	size = Vector2(120, 40)
	print("(120, 40) => ", size)
	size = Vector2(121, 40)
	print("(121, 40) => ", size)

func print_new():
	print("Uh Oh! I was resized!!!")
```
I put this on a random control in the scene tree and set up a graph node as below. 
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3004d662-217b-49f8-99d0-0bb992c0cc5a)

I connected the `resize` signal to `print_new()`
You can see that it passes #100112 as the output is as expected, and the size is set properly.  (See final image)

In 4.4 (stable), this bug exists.
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/62d6b288-f0d2-46eb-a301-53cf6b225b7e)

---

Upon scrolling (I did not take a video due to my computer being too weak to take videos), and zooming in and out, even up to extreme limits, the `print_new()` was never called. 
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/eacdd29a-18ec-4a5c-b521-da07426a1c21)

*Bugsquad edit:* Fixes https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/100112

summary: Optimize startup times by avoiding loading fonts twice.

description.text:
This change decreases editor startup time by ~0.44s (10%).

I believe this fixes a regression introduced by #61772 (so technically a `4.0` regression).

It should be possible to cherry-pick this change to earlier versions, but it's technically a behavior change (fonts that were previously loaded may error now, if an invalid font face index was provided). I'll let folks be the judge if that's worth it.

I tested loading fonts with an invalid font face index. It returns early from the function and prints an error, as expected.

## Explanation
When prompted to load a font, `TextServerAdv` first finds the number of available faces in the font by passing `-1` into `FT_Open_Face`. This feature is [documented](https://freetype.org/freetype2/docs/reference/ft2-face_creation.html#ft_open_face) in the function's description. Unfortunately, the function description does not mention that the function still fully loads the font. In effect, this means we've been loading fonts twice as often as needed. 

## Benchmark
I profiled a ~0.44s decrease in editor startup time:
```
 ❯ hyperfine -m25 -iw1 "bin/godot.macos.editor.arm64.master --path /Users/lukas/dev/godot/empty-game --editor --quit" "bin/godot.macos.editor.arm64 --path /Users/lukas/dev/godot/empty-game --editor --quit"
Benchmark 1: bin/godot.macos.editor.arm64.master --path /Users/lukas/dev/godot/empty-game --editor --quit
  Time (mean ± σ):      4.738 s ±  0.146 s    [User: 3.250 s, System: 0.400 s]
  Range (min … max):    4.357 s …  4.925 s    25 runs

Benchmark 2: bin/godot.macos.editor.arm64 --path /Users/lukas/dev/godot/empty-game --editor --quit
  Time (mean ± σ):      4.297 s ±  0.093 s    [User: 2.827 s, System: 0.394 s]
  Range (min … max):    4.133 s …  4.386 s    25 runs
```

## Addendum
Font loading is still a large contributor to editor startup time (6%). It may be possible to further decrease the amount of times fonts are loaded. But this would require a more invasive change.
